perturbation.original_text: 1.1 The Intellectual Property 1.1.1 Upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth, Party A hereby agrees to grant and Party B hereby agree to accept the right to use the intellectual property in PRC. The license under this Agreement is a non-exclusive, non-assignable and non-transferable license. 1.1.2 Party A owns the sole and exclusive right of the intellectual property, including any improvement, upgrades and derived products, no matter whether such products are created by Party A or Party B. The right and obligation under 1.1.2 of this article shall survive upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 The Intellectual Property 1.1.1 Upon the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth, Party A hereby agrees to grant and Party B hereby agree to accept the right to use the intellectual property in PRC. The license under this Agreement is a non-exclusive license. The license is assignable with written consent from Party A but remains non-transferable. 1.1.2 Party A owns the sole and exclusive right of the intellectual property, including any improvement, upgrades and derived products, no matter whether such products are created by Party A or Party B. The right and obligation under 1.1.2 of this article shall survive upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the assignability of the license. Initially, the license is explicitly non-assignable. The modified text states that 'The license is assignable with written consent from Party A'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is now unclear under what conditions, if any, Party B can assign the license, impacting Section 11, which states: 'This Agreement and all the rights and obligations of Party B hereunder shall not be assigned, pledged, sublicensed without the prior written consent of Party A'. While Section 11 is about the agreement and it's terms, not just the license, this modification makes it unclear if Section 1.1.1 takes precendence over the assignment clause in Section 11.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and all the rights and obligations of Party B hereunder shall not be assigned, pledged, sublicensed without the prior written consent of Party A.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the license is 'non-assignable'. The changed text modifies this to state that 'The license is assignable with written consent from Party A but remains non-transferable.' This directly contradicts the original condition and introduces an ambiguity that conflicts with the intent of the assignment restrictions mentioned in the contradicted text. The contradicted text says 'This Agreement and all the rights and obligations of Party B hereunder shall not be assigned, pledged, sublicensed without the prior written consent of Party A.' The change to Section 1.1.1 creates a conflict regarding assignment.

perturbation.original_text: Party A has the right to exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount of the License Fees set forth in Appendix 2 according to the actual circumstances from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: Party A only has the right to exempt the obligation of payment of the License Fees set forth in Appendix 2, but cannot adjust the amount of the License Fees under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction to Party A's rights. The original text allows Party A to both exempt payment obligations AND adjust the amount of the license fees. The modified text removes Party A's right to adjust the amount of fees but retains the right to exempt payment entirely. The contradiction is due to the limitation placed on Party A's rights, which could lead to disputes, when reffering to section 6.1, when is says 'However, Party A and Party B shall review this Agreement every six (6) months to determine whether any amendment or supplement to the Agreement is necessary by considering the circumstances after the executing of this Agreement.', which creates an ambiguity for the adjustment of payments under section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement has been duly executed as of the date first set forth above and shall be effective simultaneously. The term of this Agreement is five (5) years unless the early termination in accordance with this Agreement. However, Party A and Party B shall review this Agreement every six (6) months to determine whether any amendment or supplement to the Agreement is necessary by considering the circumstances after the executing of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted Party A the right to both exempt payment obligations and adjust license fees. The changed text removes the right to adjust fees. The contradicted text states that the parties will review the agreement every six months to determine if any amendment or supplement is necessary. Since the changed text removes Party A's right to adjust fees, it directly contradicts the spirit and intention of the review clause (the contradicted text) which is about modifying the agreement as circumstances change. This creates a strong contradiction because the parties agreed to review for adjustments which are now disallowed by the updated text.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party A may terminate this Agreement at any time with a written notice to Party B 30 days before such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party A may only terminate this Agreement if Party B breaches the agreement and with a written notice to Party B 90 days before such termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction regarding the conditions and time period under which Party A can terminate the agreement. The original clause states Party A can terminate 'at any time' with '30 days' notice. The revised clause restricts this, allowing termination 'only if Party B breaches' with '90 days' notice. This new condition directly contradicts the 'at any time' clause, leading to ambiguity about the grounds for termination. The new condition directly conflicts with section 6.1 'This Agreement has been duly executed as of the date first set forth above and shall be effective simultaneously. The term of this Agreement is five (5) years unless the early termination in accordance with this Agreement.', where the early termination term is only according to the original agreement and this new condition of breach does not match up with the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement has been duly executed as of the date first set forth above and shall be effective simultaneously. The term of this Agreement is five (5) years unless the early termination in accordance with this Agreement. However, Party A and Party B shall review this Agreement every six (6) months to determine whether any amendment or supplement to the Agreement is necessary by considering the circumstances after the executing of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text allows Party A to terminate 'at any time' with 30 days' notice. The changed text restricts this to 'only if Party B breaches' with 90 days' notice, directly conflicting with the original 'at any time' clause. This new condition also contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'The term of this Agreement is five (5) years unless the early termination in accordance with this Agreement.' The change narrows the conditions of early termination, creating an inconsistency and uncertainty about the grounds for termination of the agreement.
